name: Quit Applications
description: Quit running macOS applications from Coco AI: quit the frontmost app, restart the frontmost app, or quit every other foreground app at once. Ported from the Raycast Quit Applications extension by mackopes (MIT).

commands.name: Quit Frontmost Application
commands.description: Quit the application that is currently in the foreground.

args: osascript -e 'try' -e 'tell application "System Events" to set fm to name of first application process whose frontmost is true' -e 'tell application fm to quit' -e 'end try'

commands.name: Restart Frontmost Application
commands.description: Quit the application that is currently in the foreground and immediately reactivate it.

args: osascript -e 'try' -e 'tell application "System Events" to set fm to name of first application process whose frontmost is true' -e 'tell application fm' -e 'repeat while its running' -e 'quit' -e 'delay 0.5' -e 'end repeat' -e 'activate' -e 'end tell' -e 'end try'

commands.name: Quit All Other Applications
commands.description: Quit every other foreground application (Finder is left alone). The app calling this command may also be quit.

args: osascript -e 'try' -e 'tell application "System Events" to set procs to name of every process whose background only is false and name is not "Finder"' -e 'repeat with p in procs' -e 'try' -e 'tell application (p as string) to quit' -e 'end try' -e 'end repeat' -e 'end try'
